zh.lifeSummary: 當Richenza von Berg於大約1092年出生在德國巴登-爾特波格阿爾-多瑙-可萊斯爾可林根時，她的父親Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen24歲，母親Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg20歲。她於1110年與Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1125年9月27日享年34歲，埋葬在德國薩辛可萊斯 普勞賴興巴赫。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richenza von Berg于大约1092年出生在德国巴登-爾特波格阿爾-多瑙-可萊斯爾可林根时，她的父亲Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen24岁，母亲Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg20岁。她于1110年与Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1125年9月27日享年34岁，埋葬在德国薩辛可萊斯 普勞賴興巴赫。

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Berg
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Berger
meanings: German, Dutch, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): topographic name for someone who lived in the mountains or hills (see   Berg  ). The surname of German origin is also found in many other European countries, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Russia, Poland, Czechia, Hungary, and Croatia, and Slovenia, often as a translation into German of corresponding Slavic topographic names or surnames. As a Jewish name it is mainly artificial. Compare   Bargar  ,   Barger  ,   Barker  ,   Barrier  , and   Barriger  .
French: occupational name from Old French bergier ‘shepherd’ (from Late Latin berbicarius, from berbex ‘ram’). It is also found in England, as a surname of Huguenot origin. Compare Shepard .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms so named with the plural of   Berg   ‘mountain’.

variants.name: Bergh
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Johan, Anders, Arndt, Owe, Per, Sigvard, Sven, Syvert.
Scandinavian: variant of   Berg  .
Dutch and English: topographic name denoting someone living on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe, Middle English bergh ‘hill’ (Old English beorg). Compare Dutch Berg and Vandenbergh .

variants.name: Vandenberg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Dirk, Marinus, Gerrit, Gerritt, Hendrik, Jacobus, Maarten, Pieter, Wim, Albertus, Durk, Hendrick.
Dutch (Van den Berg) and Flemish (also Van den Berg): topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill, Middle Dutch berghe. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berglin
meanings: Swedish: variant of Berg + the suffix -lin, derived from Latin -(l)inius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berget
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Knut.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of several farm names especially in eastern Norway named with the definite singular form of berg ‘mountain, hill’ (see Berg ).
French: topographic name, a derivative of   Berge   ‘river bank’.

variants.name: Baerg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Gerhard, Kurt.
German (Bärg): variant of Berg .

variants.name: Bergen
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berghorst
meanings: Dutch (Gelderland): topographic name for someone living on or by a hill with shrubberies (see   Berg   and   Horst  ).

variants.name: Barg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Erwin, Hans, Mathias, Wilfried.
North German:
topographic name for someone who lived on a hill, Low German barg. Compare Berg .

variants.name: Berge
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Maren, Johan, Karsten, Alf, Bent, Erik, Gudmund, Knud, Peer, Per, Sten, Thor.
Norwegian and Swedish: variant of   Berg  , from the dative form. As a Norwegian surname, this is a habitational name from any of numerous farms so named all over the country.
German: topographic name for a person by or on a hill, from an extended form of Berg (formerly with a preposition with dative case, e.g. am Berge).

en.lifeSummary: When Richenza von Berg was born about 1092, in Schelklingen, Alb-Donau-Kreis, Baden-Württemberg, Germany, her father, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, was 24 and her mother, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, was 20. She married Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN in 1110. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 27 September 1125, at the age of 34, and was buried in Reichenbach, Kreis Plauen, Saxony, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richenza von Berg est née environ 1092, à Schelklingen, Alb-Donau-Kreis, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN en 1110. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 27 septembre 1125, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Reichenbach, Kreis Plauen, Sachsen, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richenza von Berg um 1092 in Schelklingen, Alb-Donau-Kreis, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen 24 und ihre Mutter Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg 20. Sie hat 1110 Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 27. September 1125 im Alter von 34 Jahren und wurde in Reichenbach, Kreis Plauen, Sachsen, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richenza von Berg è nata il circa 1092 nel Schelklingen, Alb-Donau-Kreis, Baden-Württemberg, Germania, sua padre, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN nel 1110. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 27 settembre 1125, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a Reichenbach, Kreis Plauen, Sachsen, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Richenza von Bergが約1092年にドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグアルブ-ドナウ-クレイスシェルクリンジェンで生まれた時，父Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingenは24歳，母Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburgは20歳であった。1110年にVladislaus I VON BÖHMENと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1125年9月27日に34歳で死去し，ドイツサチセンクレイス プラウンレイチェンバチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richenza von Berg(이)가 대략 1092년 독일 바데-wu르테버그 알브-도나-크레이스 셸클링에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1110년 Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1125년 9월 27일 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 작센 크레이스 플로언 라이천바치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richenza von Berg nasceu aproximadamente 1092, em Schelklingen, Alb-Donau-Kreis, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN em 1110. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 27 de setembro de 1125, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Reichenbach, Kreis Plauen, Sachsen, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richenza von Berg родилась около 1092 г. в местности щелклинген, алб-донау-крис, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия, её отцу по имени Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN в 1110 г. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 27 сентября 1125 г. в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности раичэнбач, крис плауен, сачсен, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richenza von Berg nació aproximadamente 1092, en Schelklingen, Alb-Donau-Kreis, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Count Heinrich von Berg of Berg-Schelklingen, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Adelaide von Mochental von Vohburg, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Vladislaus I VON BÖHMEN en 1110. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió el 27 de septiembre de 1125, a la edad de 34 años, y fue sepultada en Reichenbach, Kreis Plauen, Sachsen, Alemania.
